controls.title: The organizational mission is understood and informs cybersecurity risk management
controls.description: The organization's mission and strategic priorities are documented and used to inform cybersecurity risk decisions and resource allocation.

controls.title: Critical objectives, capabilities, and services that stakeholders depend on are understood and communicated
controls.description: The organization identifies and documents critical functions, services and dependencies that drive cybersecurity priorities.

controls.title: Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
controls.description: A formal cybersecurity risk management strategy is documented, approved by leadership, and reviewed periodically.

controls.title: Roles, responsibilities, and authorities related to cybersecurity risk management are established, communicated, understood, and enforced
controls.description: Cybersecurity roles (CISO, security officers, asset owners) and accountabilities are formally defined and communicated organization-wide.

controls.title: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is established based on organizational context, cybersecurity strategy, and priorities
controls.description: Comprehensive cybersecurity policies are documented, approved by leadership, and reviewed at least annually.

controls.title: A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program, strategy, objectives, policies, and processes are established
controls.description: A formal C-SCRM program is established with documented strategy, governance, and integration into procurement and vendor management.

controls.title: Requirements to address cybersecurity risks in supply chains are established, prioritized, and integrated into contracts and other types of agreements
controls.description: Cybersecurity requirements are explicitly included in vendor contracts, SLAs and procurement processes.

controls.title: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained
controls.description: A current, accurate inventory of physical and virtual hardware assets exists and is actively maintained.

controls.title: Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained
controls.description: A current inventory of software (including SaaS), services and systems exists, including version and lifecycle status.

controls.title: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded
controls.description: Systematic vulnerability assessment processes identify, validate and track vulnerabilities across all assets.

controls.domain: Protect — Identity Management, Authentication & Access Control
controls.title: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
controls.description: Identity lifecycle management covers provisioning, modification and de-provisioning of all human and non-human identities.

controls.domain: Protect — Identity Management, Authentication & Access Control
controls.title: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated
controls.description: Strong authentication, including multi-factor where appropriate, is enforced for all access to organizational resources.

controls.domain: Protect — Identity Management, Authentication & Access Control
controls.title: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties
controls.description: Access rights follow least privilege and SoD principles, with documented policy and periodic access reviews.

controls.title: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected
controls.description: Data-at-rest is protected with encryption, integrity controls and access restrictions appropriate to sensitivity.

controls.title: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected
controls.description: Data-in-transit is encrypted using current cryptographic standards across all communication channels.

controls.title: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
controls.description: Network monitoring tools (NDR, IDS/IPS, NetFlow analysis) provide continuous visibility and alerting on anomalous activity.

controls.title: Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events
controls.description: Endpoint detection and response (EDR), runtime application protection, and configuration monitoring detect anomalous host activity.

controls.title: The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared
controls.description: A tested incident response plan is executed promptly upon incident declaration, with coordinated activation of internal teams and external parties (legal, regulators, vendors).

controls.title: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident
controls.description: Forensic analysis and root cause investigation are performed on incidents to enable effective response and lessons learned.

controls.title: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated from the incident response process
controls.description: Documented and tested recovery procedures restore systems and data following an incident, validated against RPO/RTO targets.

controls.title: Recovery activities and progress in restoring operational capabilities are communicated to designated internal and external stakeholders
controls.description: Stakeholder communications during recovery follow a documented plan covering internal teams, customers, regulators and public communications as appropriate.
